P.02
Colorado Design
Build Fly (AIAA)
2025 - 2026
CU Boulder
Flight Test Day: Colorado DBF Team The full Colorado Design Build Fly team gathered at the airfield following a successful prototype flight test. Field validation sessions like this are essential for tuning our aerodynamic models and building pilot confidence before competition.
Prototype: Systems Layout Top-down view of our "Pink Foam" rapid prototype. This low-fidelity model verified the placement of dual propulsion motors, ESCs, servos, and the banner-release mechanism before committing to expensive composite materials.
Pre-Flight Integration Check Collaborating with the avionics and structures leads to verify control surface mixing, servo throws, and structural integrity of the airframe immediately before a test flight at the airfield.
Mission Verification: Aircraft in Flight The prototype successfully airborne during a validation lap. For the 2026 AIAA competition, the team needs to demonstrate sustained flight while towing a banner and carrying payload. This flight confirmed our propulsion system hits the required thrust-to-weight ratio.
Propulsion Engineer

On the Colorado Design Build Fly team competing in the international AIAA DBF competition. The 2025-2026 challenge was to build a Banner-Towing Bush Plane with short takeoff, banner deployment, and payload mission requirements.

  • Conducted static thrust testing on various motor and propeller combinations, verifying performance against thrust-to-weight ratio calculations for high-drag banner-tow missions.
  • Analyzed battery discharge curves to optimize energy density for multiple flight laps while minimizing airframe weight.
  • Collaborated with Structures to design and fabricate a custom Landing Gear Test Pad for empirical durability testing against hard landings.
  • Led propulsion avionics integration into the fuselage, ensuring proper cooling, CG placement, and reliable power distribution.
  • Hands-on rapid prototyping using foam cutting and composite techniques to build a lightweight and repairable airframe.
